round0_result.summary: Round-0 carried one hypothesis (aflfast_nfuzz_damping_starves_deep_path) over both branches and marked it decidable:false: the AFLFast FAST n_fuzz damping is a runtime scheduling dynamic with no per-branch corpus fingerprint, the campaign emits no per-seed energy/n_fuzz traces, and both branches are libxml2 (no on-disk corpus on s4). No rule could fire.
round0_result.interpretation: Round-0 did NOT under-explore. It correctly identified that the discriminating quantity (per-seed energy scaled by campaign-wide bucket hit-frequency) is unbuildable from current instrumentation. Round-1 re-examines all four BUILT tools the brief flagged as untried pivots and confirms each is blind to this mechanism; the verdict stands as whole-shape decidable:false.

why_decidable_false.core: aflfast_rarity (FAST PowerSchedule) differs from minimizer (PowerSchedule None) in exactly ONE thing: FAST multiplies a seed's energy by an n_fuzz log2 RARITY factor computed from how often that seed's coverage bucket has been hit CAMPAIGN-WIDE over time. On a frequently-hit (saturated) bucket the factor damps energy to ~0.4x, starving the deep path. The mutator vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers (data_realities #1) and coverage-feedback admission is identical, so the advantage is a campaign-DYNAMICS property (how much energy a seed earned, gated by its bucket's running hit-history), NOT a property of the terminal saved corpus.
why_decidable_false.even_more_scheduling_bound_than_calibrat: calibrated_energy_WL (the established whole-shape decidable:false precedent) was already non-discriminable because its advantage is per-seed energy x time. aflfast_rarity is STRICTER: its damping factor is a function of campaign-wide cumulative bucket hit-frequency (n_fuzz history), a pure runtime trajectory quantity. No terminal-corpus snapshot encodes 'how saturated was this bucket when the seed was scheduled' -- the very input to the damping is a time-series that no built tool reads.
why_decidable_false.tools_are_terminal_only: All four BUILT tools read TERMINAL corpus / seed-set state and none observes per-seed energy-over-time or campaign-wide bucket hit-history. A terminal snapshot reports WHAT the corpus contains, never HOW MUCH (or HOW RARELY-scheduled) energy produced it.
why_decidable_false.g2_independence_is_unsatisfiable: The only terminal observable that WOULD separate fast from minimizer (minimizer reached the deep gate, fast did not) is the resolve outcome itself -- the label-SOURCE, not an independent verification-SIGNAL. Verifying 'fast starved the path' with 'fast blocked' violates G2. A would-be energy_share / n_fuzz-trace descriptor is unbuilt, absent from tool_registry.json, and would re-state the technique by definition (a lower energy share on the damped bucket is what FAST DOES), again straining G2.

built_tool_pivot_disposition.reason: Both gates lack a fixed target literal: 6414 is a hash-equality on a dict-interned name3 pointer (operand_kind none_structural); 7146 is a non-zero predicate `if (val != 0)` -- the WINNING side needs val != 0, i.e. ANY non-zero byte, not a specific literal to enrich on. No fixed-offset value gate. The scheduling damping leaves no byte fingerprint regardless. Also data_realities #4: no on-disk libxml2 corpus on s4.

This is precisely the calibrated_energy_WL refuted pivot. Equal admission + identical mutators mean minimizer's and fast's terminal corpora reach structurally-comparable DTD seeds -- minimizer just got there with more sustained energy. size_lift ~ 1.0 / tag_lift ~ 1.0 are the EXPECTED terminal readings even when the live scheduling advantage was real. No joint/assembly necessity here -- single technique (aflfast_rarity), no vpc arm.

Neither gate is a Hamming/magnitude distance to an operand literal. 7146 is `val != 0` (presence of any non-zero char after the name-char/blank loops -- not a distance to a target value); 6414 is pointer equality on an interned name3. No vp-gradient operand. Also no on-disk libxml2 seeds on s4.

depth_reach reads TERMINAL per-seed exec count + blocked-side count of the branch line. The discriminating quantity for aflfast_rarity is the CAMPAIGN-WIDE bucket hit-frequency (n_fuzz history) that scales energy -- not how deep a terminal seed executes per-run. A seed that reaches the gate looks identical under depth_reach whether fast or minimizer produced it; the difference is how many MUTATION ITERATIONS the schedule afforded that seed over time, which depth_reach does not observe. Also needs the libxml2 cov image + on-disk seeds, absent on s4 (data_realities #4).

Same equal-admission argument: corpus size / token counts converge between fast and minimizer at terminal because both admit by identical coverage rules with identical mutators; the schedule only changes the RATE of accumulation, not the terminal content. No fast-vs-minimizer ratio survives.

per_member_disposition.reason: scheduling-only effect: FAST n_fuzz damping starves the saturated xmlHashLookup3 hash-lookup bucket (none_structural pointer-equality gate). Terminal-corpus invisible; the damping input (campaign-wide bucket hit-history) is a time-series no built tool reads. ALSO no on-disk libxml2 corpus on s4 (data_realities #4), so seed_set descriptors cannot score it regardless.

scheduling-only effect: FAST n_fuzz damping exhausts mutation budget on the frequently-hit attribute-validation path before a value lands val!=0 at valid.c:3807 (non-zero predicate, no target literal). Terminal-corpus invisible; the win is mutation-budget-over-time, not corpus content. ALSO no on-disk libxml2 corpus on s4.

merges_and_collapses.round0_hypothesis_kept_as_non_discrimina: The single round-0 hypothesis (aflfast_nfuzz_damping_starves_deep_path) is retained as the mechanism STORY but stays decidable:false. It was not refuted as a WRONG mechanism -- the signatures' attribution (FAST rarity damping starves a saturated deep path) is plausible -- but it is NON-DISCRIMINABLE from minimizer with any BUILT terminal-corpus tool. Per job 2 + guardrail G3, an honest non-discriminable verdict beats a rule the data cannot support.
merges_and_collapses.no_subgroup_survives: The two branches share ONE mechanism (n_fuzz energy damping on a saturated bucket) and are not separable from each other; neither yields a discriminable terminal signal. No sub-shape is rescued. NO new mechanism was invented to force a label (G3).

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence descriptor proposed (I2S untagged; aflfast_rarity is a scheduling technique, equally untagged in mutation_op).
No byte-alignment / value-distance descriptor proposed on a structural / non-zero-predicate gate.

Both branches are libxml2: no on-disk corpus on s4 -- but per cross-server rule #4 they are marked decidable:false on the MECHANISM (scheduling, terminal-corpus invisible), NOT merely on corpus absence; the verdict holds for ALL servers including the owning server (sB), since no terminal observable is independent of the resolve pattern for a pure power-schedule effect.
notes: WHOLE-SHAPE decidable:false (honest non-discriminable), confirming the round-0 verdict after re-examining all built tools. aflfast_rarity is a power-SCHEDULING technique whose ONLY difference from minimizer is the FAST n_fuzz RARITY factor: energy scaled by campaign-wide bucket hit-frequency over time. This is even more scheduling-bound than the calibrated_energy_WL precedent (also whole-shape decidable:false): the damping input is itself a runtime time-series (n_fuzz history), so no terminal-corpus snapshot can encode it. Equal coverage-admission + byte-identical mutators mean minimizer's terminal corpus reaches structurally/value-comparable seeds (just with sustained energy the saturated path was denied), so every built tool (operand_enrichment, joint_necessity, value_distance_reached, depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio, token_count) reads no fast-vs-minimizer lift -- each is dispositioned 'blind' above with a per-tool reason. The only would-be discriminator is a per-seed energy / n_fuzz-trace descriptor (energy_share / plateau_time over seed_lineage|exec_stats|coverage_timeseries) which (a) is UNBUILT and absent from tool_registry.json, and (b) would re-state the technique rather than independently verify it, straining G2. This is the spec's non-local / non-synthesizable scheduling case (CLAUDE.md benchmark-pivot: scheduling is a whole-target+campaign property, not capturable from corpus data); an honest decidable:false here is the CORRECT, paper-relevant outcome (G3), not a failure. Both branches route to fallback:inconclusive. NO hypothesis invented to force a label.
